steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause with an appositive phrase ('the site with the highest elevation'). After the blank is a second independent clause (“it had shifted to 16.8 °C by 11:00 p.m.”). The blank must join two independent clauses correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — elevation and: lacks a comma to close the appositive phrase before the coordinating conjunction, so the sentence structure breaks down. ✗
  • Choice B — elevation: leaves the appositive phrase unclosed and creates an ungrammatical run-on before “it had shifted.” ✗
  • Choice C — elevation,: closes the appositive phrase but leaves two independent clauses joined only by a comma, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — elevation, and: A comma closes the appositive, and “and” correctly joins the two independent clauses. ✓
That matches Choice D — elevation, and.
